commands.review_summary:
This PR updates manual-login Chrome reuse to clean stale profile state when no DevTools target is discoverable or a discovered target is unreachable, and adds a regression test for the dead recorded PID/no target path.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from a live run: current main returns null before cleanup when no DevTools port and no matching target are found, while the PR test encodes that stale chrome.pid/no-target path. I did not run the test because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human review/proof rather than a repair lane: I found no discrete code defect, but this external PR changes profile lock cleanup and lacks real behavior proof.

target.title: [codex] Clean stale manual-login Chrome profile locks
target.body:
## Summary

- Clean stale Chrome profile lock files when manual-login reuse finds a recorded Oracle Chrome PID but no reachable DevTools target.
- Also clean stale state when a discovered profile-matching Chrome DevTools target cannot be probed.
- Add a focused regression test for the dead-PID/no-DevTools-target path.

## Why

Manual-login mode reuses a persistent Chrome profile. Oracle records a Chrome PID and DevTools state so later runs can reuse the same browser. If that Oracle-launched Chrome exits uncleanly, the profile can be left with `SingletonLock`, `SingletonSocket`, `SingletonCookie`, or `lockfile` entries plus a dead `chrome.pid`, while `DevToolsActivePort` is missing and process discovery finds no reachable target.

Before this patch, `maybeReuseRunningChrome()` returned `null` in that path without calling `cleanupStaleProfileState()`. The next launch then had to start Chrome against a profile that still looked locked by a dead process. The existing cleanup path only covered the different case where `DevToolsActivePort` existed but was unreachable.

This keeps the existing safety rule: lock files are removed only when the recorded Oracle Chrome PID is dead, and `cleanupStaleProfileState()` still checks for another Chrome process using the profile before deleting locks.

## Validation

- `pnpm test tests/browser/reuseChrome.test.ts`
- `pnpm run format:check`
- `pnpm run typecheck`
target.author: derekszen
